claim: No Stable, release, conformance, or manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.
summary: A semantic visual action group whose opt-in toolbar mode implements one-tab-stop roving focus.
semantics: Group mode preserves each child action as a native tab stop.
Toolbar mode assigns exactly one recognized enabled action as a tab stop during SSR and every render, then implements Home, End, orientation arrows, disabled skipping, and RTL spatial arrows.
Router and custom action components may opt in through markButtonGroupAction when they forward toolbar props to exactly one native action root.
Non-interactive separators remain unmanaged; unsupported custom or nested-action children are inert so they cannot add uncontrolled toolbar tab stops.
No button or link semantics are rewritten by the group.
keyboardHint exists only in toolbar mode, becomes visible descriptive text, and is absent when omitted.

preferences.forcedColors: System colors preserve boundaries, state, and focus.
preferences.reducedMotion: Feedback remains understandable when transitions and rotation are removed.
preferences.rtl: Logical properties and the documented spatial keyboard model follow direction.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Wrap, reflow, or expose native scrolling without clipping controls or focus indicators.
